self = <tests.api_full_response.test_get_score_api.TestFullResponseGetScoreAPI testMethod=test_get_score_api_by_wrong_address> _make_id = <MagicMock name='_make_id' id='4362447440'>
def test_get_score_api_by_wrong_address(self, _make_id): with requests_mock.Mocker() as m: wrong_address = "cxb0776ee37f5b45bfaea8cff1d8232fbb6122ec32"
response_json: dict = { "jsonrpc": "2.0", "error": { "code": -32002, "message": f"SCORE not found: {wrong_address}" }, "id": 1234 } m.post(self.matcher, json=response_json, status_code=400) > result_dict = self.icon_service.get_score_api(wrong_address, full_response=True) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
tests/api_full_response/test_get_score_api.py:64: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ iconsdk/icon_service.py:178: in get_score_api return self.__provider.make_request('icx_getScoreApi', params, full_response) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ iconsdk/providers/http_provider.py:153: in make_request response_data = self._make_post_request(request_url, rpc_dict, **self._get_request_kwargs()) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
request_url = 'http://localhost:9000/api/v3' data = {'id': 1234, 'jsonrpc': '2.0', 'method': 'icx_getScoreApi', 'params': {'address': 'cxb0776ee37f5b45bfaea8cff1d8232fbb6122ec32'}} kwargs = {'headers': {'Content-Type': 'application/json'}, 'timeout': 10} buffer = <_io.BytesIO object at 0x104a51d00> c = <pycurl.Curl object at 0x11c938a30> headers = {'Content-Type': 'application/json'}
@staticmethod def _make_post_request(request_url: str, data: dict, **kwargs) -> bytes: kwargs.setdefault('timeout', 10) buffer = BytesIO() c = pycurl.Curl() c.setopt(c.URL, request_url) c.setopt(c.POST, 1) c.setopt(c.POSTFIELDS, json.dumps(data)) headers = kwargs.get('headers', {}) if headers: c.setopt(c.HTTPHEADER, [f"{key}: {value}" for key, value in headers.items()]) c.setopt(c.WRITEDATA, buffer) c.setopt(c.TIMEOUT, kwargs['timeout']) > c.perform() E pycurl.error: (7, 'Failed to connect to localhost port 9000 after 0 ms: Could not connect to server')
iconsdk/providers/http_provider.py:135: error

self = <tests.api_get.test_get_block_by_height.TestGetBlockByHeight testMethod=test_get_block_by_wrong_height> _make_id = <MagicMock name='_make_id' id='4374147152'>
def test_get_block_by_wrong_height(self, _make_id): with requests_mock.Mocker() as m: wrong_height = 5 expected_request = { 'id': 1234, 'jsonrpc': '2.0', 'method': 'icx_getBlockByHeight', 'params': { 'height': hex(wrong_height) } } response_json = { "jsonrpc": "2.0", "error": { "code": -32602, "message": "fail wrong block hash" }, "id": 1234 } m.post(self.matcher, json=response_json, status_code=400) > self.assertRaises(JSONRPCException, self.icon_service.get_block, wrong_height)
tests/api_get/test_get_block_by_height.py:100: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ iconsdk/icon_service.py:109: in get_block result = self.__provider.make_request(method, params, full_response) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ iconsdk/providers/http_provider.py:153: in make_request response_data = self._make_post_request(request_url, rpc_dict, **self._get_request_kwargs()) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
@staticmethod def _make_post_request(request_url: str, data: dict, **kwargs) -> bytes: kwargs.setdefault('timeout', 10) buffer = BytesIO() c = pycurl.Curl() c.setopt(c.URL, request_url) c.setopt(c.POST, 1) c.setopt(c.POSTFIELDS, json.dumps(data)) headers = kwargs.get('headers', {}) if headers: c.setopt(c.HTTPHEADER, [f"{key}: {value}" for key, value in headers.items()]) c.setopt(c.WRITEDATA, buffer) c.setopt(c.TIMEOUT, kwargs['timeout']) > c.perform() E pycurl.error: (7, 'Failed to connect to localhost port 9000 after 0 ms: Could not connect to server')

self = <tests.api_get.test_get_transaction_result.TestGetTransactionResult testMethod=test_get_transaction_result_wrong_hash> _make_id = <MagicMock name='_make_id' id='4374268112'>
def test_get_transaction_result_wrong_hash(self, _make_id): wrong_tx_hash: str = "0xb903239f8543d04b5dc1ba6579132b143087c68db1b2168786408fcbce568238" with requests_mock.Mocker() as m: expected_request = { 'id': 1234, 'jsonrpc': '2.0', 'method': 'icx_getTransactionResult', 'params': { 'txHash': wrong_tx_hash } } response_json = { "jsonrpc": "2.0", "error": { "code": -32602, "message": "Invalid params txHash" }, "id": 1234 } m.post(self.matcher, json=response_json, status_code=400) # case 4: when tx_hash is invalid - not exist > self.assertRaises(JSONRPCException, self.icon_service.get_transaction_result, wrong_tx_hash)
tests/api_get/test_get_transaction_result.py:95: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ iconsdk/icon_service.py:211: in get_transaction_result result = self.__provider.make_request('icx_getTransactionResult', params, full_response) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ iconsdk/providers/http_provider.py:153: in make_request response_data = self._make_post_request(request_url, rpc_dict, **self._get_request_kwargs()) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
@staticmethod def _make_post_request(request_url: str, data: dict, **kwargs) -> bytes: kwargs.setdefault('timeout', 10) buffer = BytesIO() c = pycurl.Curl() c.setopt(c.URL, request_url) c.setopt(c.POST, 1) c.setopt(c.POSTFIELDS, json.dumps(data)) headers = kwargs.get('headers', {}) if headers: c.setopt(c.HTTPHEADER, [f"{key}: {value}" for key, value in headers.items()]) c.setopt(c.WRITEDATA, buffer) c.setopt(c.TIMEOUT, kwargs['timeout']) > c.perform() E pycurl.error: (7, 'Failed to connect to localhost port 9000 after 0 ms: Could not connect to server')

testcase.failure:
self = <tests.api_send.test_send_message.TestSendMessage testMethod=test_invalid> _make_id = <MagicMock name='_make_id' id='4373336272'>
def test_invalid(self, _make_id): # When data is not hex string with requests_mock.Mocker() as m: message_transaction = MessageTransactionBuilder() \ .from_(self.setting["from"]) \ .to(self.setting["to"]) \ .step_limit(self.setting["step_limit"]) \ .nid(self.setting["nid"]) \ .nonce(self.setting["nonce"]) \ .data("test")
# Raise DataTypeException self.assertRaises(DataTypeException, message_transaction.build)
response_json = { "jsonrpc": "2.0", "error": { "code": -32602, "message": "Server error" }, "id": 1234 } m.post(self.matcher, json=response_json, status_code=400) # When address is wrong message_transaction = MessageTransactionBuilder().from_(self.setting["from"]).to(self.setting["to"][2:]) \ .step_limit(self.setting["step_limit"]).nid(self.setting["nid"]).data(self.setting["data"]).build() signed_transaction = SignedTransaction(message_transaction, self.wallet) > self.assertRaises(JSONRPCException, self.icon_service.send_transaction, signed_transaction)
tests/api_send/test_send_message.py:198: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ iconsdk/icon_service.py:301: in send_transaction return self.__provider.make_request('icx_sendTransaction', params, full_response) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ iconsdk/providers/http_provider.py:153: in make_request response_data = self._make_post_request(request_url, rpc_dict, **self._get_request_kwargs()) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
@staticmethod def _make_post_request(request_url: str, data: dict, **kwargs) -> bytes: kwargs.setdefault('timeout', 10) buffer = BytesIO() c = pycurl.Curl() c.setopt(c.URL, request_url) c.setopt(c.POST, 1) c.setopt(c.POSTFIELDS, json.dumps(data)) headers = kwargs.get('headers', {}) if headers: c.setopt(c.HTTPHEADER, [f"{key}: {value}" for key, value in headers.items()]) c.setopt(c.WRITEDATA, buffer) c.setopt(c.TIMEOUT, kwargs['timeout']) > c.perform() E pycurl.error: (7, 'Failed to connect to localhost port 9000 after 0 ms: Could not connect to server')
